Webhooks

Webhooks send HTTP POST requests to external services when events occur (scan complete, violations found, etc.). Generic webhooks send JSON payloads; Discord, Slack, and Gotify use service-specific formatting. Read more →

A webhook is an HTTP POST Stillwater sends to a URL you provide whenever a chosen event happens (a rule fixes a violation, an artist is added, and so on). Configure as many webhooks as you need to forward Stillwater events into Slack, Discord, your home automation, or any service that can accept JSON over HTTP.

No webhooks configured.

A label that identifies this webhook in the list. Only visible within Stillwater. Read more →
The payload format Stillwater uses when it sends the request. Generic sends a JSON object; Discord, Slack, and Gotify each use a format their service expects directly. Read more →
The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint that receives the POST request. For Discord, this is a Discord webhook URL; for Slack, an incoming webhook URL; for Gotify, the application push URL. Read more →
